meta.auditor: Claude Code via Figma REST API + MCP (get_variable_defs) + cached token diff + cached doc frames
evidence_sources: Figma REST API (file structure, components, styles, variables/local) -- filtered and cached
Token parity diff (scripts/output/mui-token-diff.json, cached from v2.0)
Documentation frame reader (scripts/output/mui-doc-frames.json, cached from v2.0)
MUI default theme extraction (scripts/output/mui-default-theme.json, cached from v2.0)

conditions_for_advancement: Resolve CDC-001: replace code import snippets with functional intent descriptions on component set root entries, starting with the 6 primary component pages (Alert, Card, Avatar, Button, Checkbox, Text Field)

Resolve IQ-001: write structured intent documentation following the six-level hierarchy (purpose, structure, behaviour, use cases, error handling, edge cases) for at least the 14 components that have real descriptions
Resolve TA-001: add semantic and component token layers for spacing, typography, shape, and elevation -- currently only colour has three-layer architecture
Resolve TD-001: add descriptions to all token collections, priority: spacing (no semantic names), typography (overlapping sizes), primitives (no usage guidance)
Resolve DPG-001: create a parity gap register documenting the 7 known token value mismatches and structural gaps between Figma and code

0_prerequisites.cluster_summary: Platform strategy is implicit; an agent must infer web-only from convention rather than declaration.

0.1_platform_architecture_clarity.narrative: MUI is an explicitly single-platform web system. The strategy is implicit rather than documented in the Figma file. Breakpoints, interaction states, and component APIs all target web. An agent can infer the platform but must rely on convention rather than declaration.

1_token_and_variable_system.cluster_summary: Colour tokens are well-structured with alias chains; spacing, typography, shape, and elevation lack layered architecture and descriptions.

1.1_token_implementation.narrative: Colour tokens implemented as Figma Variables (palette: 195 vars across 2 collections, material/colors: 262 vars across 2 collections). Spacing as Variables (20 across 2 collections). Typography partially implemented (fontSize as Variables via aliases, other properties as text styles). Elevation as 24 effect styles, not Variables.

1.2_alias_chain_integrity.narrative: 125 alias references across 532 variables (23.5%). Palette collections have 99 aliases forming semantic-to-primitive chains. Typography has 26 aliases for fontSize. Spacing, shape, and breakpoints have zero alias chains -- flat direct values only.

1.3_token_architecture_depth.narrative: Primitive layer exists (material/colors: 262 vars). Semantic layer exists (palette: 195 vars). Component-level tokens partially exist (_components prefixed vars). Non-colour categories (spacing, typography, shape) have no layered architecture. Spacing is flat numeric values with no semantic or component layers.

1.4_primitive_naming.narrative: Full words and slashes throughout (blue/700, not b-700). Consistent within collections. Namespace prefixes present. Spacing uses numeric names which is reasonable for a base-unit system.

1.5_token_format_machine_readability.narrative: Code-side tokens are programmatic (createTheme()). Extraction script produces normalised JSON. Format is parseable but requires the extraction step, not natively in DTCG or Style Dictionary format.

1.6_token_documentation.narrative: 98 of 532 variables (18.4%) have descriptions. Descriptions present on palette state tokens (72) and some palette secondary collection (26). Absent on material/colors primitives (262), spacing (20), typography (40), shape (5), breakpoints (7), and metadata (2).

2_component_quality.cluster_summary: Variant coverage is excellent (1034 components, 826 stateful); token binding is inconsistent, with spacing hardcoded throughout.

2.1_component_to_token_binding.narrative: MCP get_variable_defs spot-check of Alert (Severity=Error, Variant=Filled) shows colour fills bound to palette variables (error/main, error/contrastText), typography partially bound (fontFamily, fontWeightMedium, _fontSize aliases), border radius bound to shape variable. Spacing not bound -- no spacing variables in any spot-checked component.

2.2_component_api_composability.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

2.3_variant_completeness.narrative: 1034 published components. Interactive states (Enabled, Hovered, Focused, Pressed, Disabled) consistently defined across component sets. Button has variants across multiple axes. Focus state on interactive components.

2.4_escape_hatch_usage.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

3_documentation_and_intent.cluster_summary: Critical gap: 64.2% of components have no description; of those with descriptions, 96.2% are code import snippets with zero functional intent.

3.1_component_description_coverage.narrative: 370 of 1034 components (35.8%) have any description. Of those, 356 (96.2%) are code import snippets. 14 components (1.4%) have real descriptions, all on Avatar Group variants. Functional intent coverage is approximately 0%.

3.2_documentation_structure_machine_read.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

3.3_intent_quality.narrative: Scored against the six-level documentation hierarchy (purpose, structure, behaviour, use cases, error handling, edge cases). The 14 non-code-snippet descriptions cover only level 2 (structure: 'Number variant enables custom margins'). No component covers levels 1, 3, 4, 5, or 6. No interaction patterns documented (loading, empty state, error recovery, validation, navigation, dismissal). An agent cannot make a component selection decision from any description in the file.

3.4_usage_guidance_formalisation.narrative: Code-only dimension. Not assessed in this Figma-primary audit.

3.5_documentation_frame_metadata.narrative: 6/6 page-level descriptions present (real text from *Library / Component Heading). 3/17 sub-component descriptions are real (18%). Rich structural metadata extractable: variant inventories, variant axes, boolean props, text props, instance swap props, compound components. Structure is machine-readable; intent content is absent.

4_design_quality_baseline.cluster_summary: Strong foundations (colour system, spacing scale, variant states) but missing motion tokens, empty state patterns, error recovery patterns, and help documentation.

4.1_interaction_targets.narrative: Touch and click targets meet 44px minimum on most interactive components; a small number of icon-only actions fall below threshold.

4.2_contrast_ratios.narrative: Primary and secondary palette tokens meet WCAG AA contrast against intended backgrounds; some lighter variants are borderline.

4.3_type_scale_consistency.narrative: Typography scale follows a consistent ratio; one non-standard size step exists between subtitle and body.

4.4_type_completeness.narrative: Core type styles defined; overline and subtitle2 lack full variable binding for lineHeight and letterSpacing.

4.5_spacing_scale_regularity.narrative: Spacing scale is regular with an 8px base unit across 20 variables in 2 collections.

4.6_grid_and_layout_system.narrative: Grid component exists with 12-column structure; breakpoint variables defined (7 across 2 collections) but layout tokens are not bound to a responsive system in Figma.

4.7_motion_duration_ranges.narrative: No motion duration tokens or variables in the Figma file; transitions exist only in code.

4.8_motion_easing.narrative: No easing curves defined as variables or documented in the Figma file; code defines easeInOut, easeOut, and sharp curves.

4.9_focus_state_presence.narrative: Focus state variants present on primary inputs and buttons; inconsistent on secondary interactive elements.

4.10_error_state_coverage.narrative: Error states present on form components (TextField, Select, Checkbox) with colour and helper text; missing on some secondary input components.

4.11_loading_state_coverage.narrative: CircularProgress and LinearProgress components exist; loading states not integrated as variants on Button, Card, or other content-bearing components.

4.12_empty_state_coverage.narrative: No empty state patterns or placeholder components in the Figma file.

4.13_colour_system_structure.narrative: Full Material colour system with 262 primitive colour variables, 195 semantic palette variables, and alias chains from palette to primitives.

4.14_icon_sizing_consistency.narrative: Icons follow a consistent 24px default with small and large variants; sizing is not bound to variables.

4.15_elevation_shadow_system.narrative: 24 elevation levels defined as effect styles matching the Material elevation scale; not yet migrated to Variables.

4.16_visibility_of_system_status.narrative: Snackbar, Alert, CircularProgress, and LinearProgress provide system status feedback.

4.17_match_between_system_and_real_world.narrative: Component naming follows Material Design conventions; some names not intuitive (Fab, Chip).

4.18_user_control_and_freedom.narrative: Dialog and Drawer support dismissal; undo patterns not represented as components.

4.19_consistency_and_standards.narrative: Consistent Material Design 3 patterns with uniform variant naming and state conventions.

4.20_error_prevention.narrative: Form validation states on TextField; confirmation dialogs and destructive action safeguards not formalised.

4.21_recognition_rather_than_recall.narrative: Autocomplete, Select, Menu provide recognition-based interaction patterns.

4.22_flexibility_and_efficiency.narrative: Multiple sizes and density variants; keyboard shortcut patterns not represented.

4.23_aesthetic_and_minimalist_design.narrative: Material Design information density principles followed; some compound components carry unnecessary visual complexity.

4.24_error_recovery.narrative: No error recovery patterns (retry, undo, rollback) represented as components or documented patterns.

4.25_help_and_documentation.narrative: No tooltip-based help, onboarding, or contextual documentation patterns.

4.26_visual_hierarchy.narrative: Typography scale and elevation system support hierarchy; no explicit hierarchy guidelines or emphasis level tokens.

4.27_visual_rhythm_and_proportion.narrative: 8px base unit provides rhythmic spacing; proportional relationships not formalised beyond the grid.

5_governance_and_ecosystem.cluster_summary: Naming is consistent; versioning and deprecation patterns are weak; code-only governance dimensions not assessed.

5.1_naming_convention_consistency.narrative: Tokens use slashes consistently. Components use variant=value syntax. Minor inconsistencies with internal prefixes (*Library, *Custom, _components, _fontSize).

5.2_versioning_and_changelog_discipline.narrative: Metadata variables record version. Footer text shows version on headings. No structured changelog in the Figma file.

5.4_deprecation_patterns.narrative: No deprecation markers on any component or variable in the Figma file.

6_design_to_code_parity.cluster_summary: Token value and naming parity is excellent (98%+ match); structural parity gaps are undocumented.

6.1_token_value_parity.narrative: 330 of 337 matched tokens have identical values (98%). 7 genuine mismatches: warning/main colour drift, 3 material/colors subtle drifts, h4 fontSize, fontFamily scope, breakpoints/xs semantics.

6.2_token_naming_parity.narrative: 100% naming alignment across all matched tokens.

6.3_component_naming_parity.narrative: Figma and code use identical base component names. Variant naming conventions differ syntactically (Figma: Size=Medium, code: size='medium') but are predictable.

6.4_variant_and_state_coverage_parity.narrative: Figma has comprehensive state variants matching code states. Not deeply cross-referenced at scale.

6.5_behaviour_parity.narrative: Not assessed. Requires deeper simultaneous inspection of Figma interactions and code behaviour.

6.6_documentation_of_parity_gaps.narrative: No documented parity gaps. The 7 value mismatches and structural differences are undocumented.

findings.summary: Elevation and typography sub-properties not implemented as Figma Variables
findings.description: Elevation/shadow implemented as 24 effect styles rather than Figma Variables. Typography properties beyond fontSize (lineHeight, letterSpacing) stored as text style properties rather than variables.
findings.recommendation: Migrate elevation values to Variables when Figma supports effect variables. Document the text style / variable split as an intentional architectural decision.

findings.summary: Spacing, shape, and breakpoint variables lack alias chains
findings.description: Spacing collections (20 variables across 2 collections) use flat values with no alias chain. Shape (5 variables) and breakpoints (7 variables) also lack alias chains. Only colour (99 palette aliases) and typography (26 fontSize aliases) have alias architecture.
findings.recommendation: Introduce semantic layers for spacing (e.g. spacing/inset, spacing/stack) and shape (e.g. shape/container, shape/input) aliasing to their respective primitives.

findings.summary: Three-layer token architecture exists only for colour; other categories are flat
findings.description: Three-layer architecture exists only for colour. Spacing (20 vars), typography (40 vars), shape (5 vars), and elevation (24 effect styles) lack semantic and component layers. An agent working with spacing must use raw numeric values (1=8, 2=16) with no semantic context.
findings.recommendation: Add semantic spacing tokens (spacing/inset, spacing/stack, spacing/inline) aliasing to primitives. Add component-level spacing tokens per component. Extend the pattern to typography, shape, and elevation.

findings.summary: 81.6% of Figma Variables have no description
findings.description: 81.6% of Figma Variables have no description (434 of 532). All 262 material/colors primitives, all 20 spacing variables, all 40 typography variables, all 5 shape variables, and all 7 breakpoint variables lack descriptions. Only palette collections have descriptions (98 of 195).
findings.recommendation: Add descriptions to all token collections. Priority: spacing (no semantic names, descriptions are the only context), typography (multiple overlapping size values), primitives (no usage guidance).

findings.summary: Component spacing values are hardcoded, not bound to tokens
findings.description: Spacing values hardcoded as px across components. Alert spot-check via get_variable_defs shows colour and typography bindings but no spacing token binding. No comprehensive component-level token layer for spacing.
findings.recommendation: Bind component spacing properties to spacing variables rather than hardcoded px values.

findings.summary: 96.2% of component descriptions are code snippets with zero functional intent
findings.description: 35.8% of components have any description (370 of 1034). Of those, 96.2% are code import snippets (356). Only 14 components (1.4%) have real descriptions, all on Avatar Group variants. Functional intent coverage is approximately 0%.
findings.recommendation: Replace code snippets with functional descriptions: when to use, when not to use, expected behaviour, constraints. Prioritise the 6 primary component pages (Alert, Card, Avatar, Button, Checkbox, Text Field).

findings.summary: Zero components have structured intent documentation against the six-level hierarchy
findings.description: Zero components have structured intent documentation against the six-level hierarchy. The 14 real descriptions cover only structure (level 2). No documented interaction patterns (loading, empty state, error recovery, validation, navigation, dismissal).
findings.recommendation: Write intent documentation following the six-level hierarchy for each primary component: purpose (level 1), structure (level 2), intended behaviour (level 3), main use cases (level 4). Add pattern documentation for loading, empty state, and error recovery.

findings.summary: All 6 component pages have real page-level descriptions
findings.description: All 6 component pages have real page-level descriptions from the *Library / Component Heading.
findings.recommendation: Maintain page-level descriptions as the file evolves. Consider adding intent-level content to complement the structural metadata.

findings.summary: 82% of sub-component documentation frames contain placeholder text
findings.description: 14 of 17 sub-component sections have placeholder lorem ipsum descriptions in *Library / Component Information. 18% real description coverage at the sub-component level.
findings.recommendation: Replace placeholder text with functional sub-component descriptions covering purpose and structure (levels 1-2 of the documentation hierarchy).

findings.summary: No deprecation markers on any component or variable
findings.description: No deprecation markers found on any component or variable in the Figma file.
findings.recommendation: Implement deprecation markers using variable descriptions or a naming convention (e.g. [DEPRECATED] prefix) for components and tokens that are being phased out.

findings.summary: warning/main colour value drifted between Figma and code
findings.description: warning/main colour mismatch between Figma and code: Figma resolves to a different shade than the code default theme.
findings.recommendation: Align warning/main colour value between Figma palette variable and code createTheme() default. Determine which is the source of truth and update the other.

findings.summary: 3 material/colors tokens show subtle value drift
findings.description: 3 material/colors tokens show subtle value drift between Figma variables and code defaults.
findings.recommendation: Review the 3 drifted material/colors values and determine whether the drift is intentional (design decision) or accidental (sync issue). Document the decision.

findings.summary: h4 fontSize, fontFamily scope, and breakpoints/xs semantics diverge
findings.description: h4 fontSize, fontFamily fallback scope, and breakpoints/xs semantics differ between Figma and code.
findings.recommendation: Document these as known intentional gaps in a parity register. fontFamily fallback scope is expected (Figma has no fallback stack concept). breakpoints/xs semantics may need alignment.

findings.summary: 7 token mismatches and structural parity gaps are undocumented
findings.description: 7 token value mismatches and multiple structural gaps (spacing not in Variables, typography sub-properties absent, shadows/zIndex/transitions code-only) are undocumented.
findings.recommendation: Create a parity gap register documenting known differences between Figma and code. Track which gaps are intentional and which are drift. Review and update the register with each release.

quick_wins.action: Replace placeholder lorem ipsum text in 14 of 17 sub-component documentation frames with real descriptions (DFM-002)
quick_wins.projected_score_improvement: Dimension 3.5 from 2 to 3; Cluster 3 from 16.7 to 25.0

quick_wins.action: Add descriptions to all 20 spacing variables and 40 typography variables explaining when to use each value (TD-001)
quick_wins.projected_score_improvement: Dimension 1.6 from 1 to 2; removes one blocker

quick_wins.action: Create a parity gap register documenting the 7 known token value mismatches and structural differences (DPG-001)
quick_wins.projected_score_improvement: Dimension 6.6 from 0 to 2; removes one blocker; Cluster 6 from 70.0 to 80.0

quick_wins.action: Implement deprecation markers using variable descriptions or naming convention (DEP-001)

foundational_blockers.action: Replace code import snippets with functional intent descriptions on the 6 primary component pages. Write purpose (level 1) and structure (level 2) for each component set root entry (CDC-001, IQ-001)
foundational_blockers.projected_score_improvement: Cluster 3 from 16.7 to 40+; removes two blockers. Largest single impact on overall score.

foundational_blockers.action: Build semantic and component token layers for spacing, typography, shape, and elevation. Extend the colour three-layer pattern to all categories (TA-001)
foundational_blockers.projected_score_improvement: Cluster 1 from 54.2 to 70+; removes one blocker

foundational_blockers.action: Bind component spacing properties to spacing variables instead of hardcoded px values (CTB-001)
foundational_blockers.projected_score_improvement: Dimension 2.1 from 2 to 3; Cluster 2 from 75.0 to 87.5

post_migration.action: Add motion duration and easing tokens to the Figma file, mirroring the code-side transition values
post_migration.projected_score_improvement: Cluster 4 from 56.5 to 62+; removes two blockers

post_migration.action: Create empty state and error recovery pattern components or documentation pages
Add help and contextual documentation patterns (tooltip-based help, onboarding)
Migrate elevation effect styles to Figma Variables when supported

data_gaps.description: MCP spot-check limited to one component (Alert Severity=Error Variant=Filled) via get_variable_defs. No full get_design_context calls made. Binding scores may not be representative of the full component set.
data_gaps.impact: Dimension 2.1 score could be higher or lower with broader sampling. Scores for Cluster 4 interaction targets and focus states rely on REST API data only.

data_gaps.description: Code-only dimensions (2.2, 2.4, 3.2, 3.4, 5.3, 5.5, 5.6, 5.7, 6.5) scored null. 9 of 44 dimensions not assessed.
data_gaps.impact: Cluster scores for 2, 3, 5, and 6 are calculated from available dimensions only. True scores would be lower if code-only dimensions score poorly.

data_gaps.description: Cluster 4 Tier 1 dimensions 4.7 and 4.8 (motion) scored 0 because motion tokens are absent from Figma. Code has transition values that were not scored against.
data_gaps.impact: These scores would improve to 3-4 if code-side motion values were included. Currently inflates the blocker count.

data_gaps.description: Token parity diff and documentation frame data reused from v2.0 cache. The Figma file may have been updated since those caches were produced (variable count increased from 446 to 532).
data_gaps.impact: Cluster 6 scores may not reflect the current state of parity. Token parity findings (TVP-001 through TVP-003) may be stale.

version_delta.narrative: v2.1 validation run against the same MUI Figma file. Schema restructured but scoring methodology unchanged. The Figma file has been updated since v2.0 (532 variables vs 446, 98 described vs 72) but not enough to change any dimension score by more than 1 point. All dimension scores match v2.0. Methodology note: v2.1 uses the two-phase audit approach and get_variable_defs for MCP spot-checks instead of get_design_context.
